scenes.title: Evidence Before Dots
scenes.visualIntent: Open on three real-scale evidence panels, then reveal a covered particle-model workspace beneath them; label evidence before model.

checkpoint.prompt: Which should come first when building our explanation?

scenes.title: A Solid Model
scenes.visualIntent: Show block evidence above a close-packed vibrating dot model; compare wood, rubber and a labelled porous sponge without changing state labels.

scenes.title: A Liquid Model
scenes.visualIntent: Animate water changing container shape, then align it with close sliding particles and a capped water-filled syringe; include a clear model-limit callout.

scenes.title: A Gas Model
scenes.visualIntent: Show a needle-free capped syringe beside a gas-particle box; decrease gaps as the plunger moves while particle size remains constant.

checkpoint.prompt: What changes when trapped air is compressed in this model?
checkpoint.answer: The gaps between particles become smaller.

scenes.title: Compression Comparison
scenes.visualIntent: Use side-by-side matched syringes with equal gentle-force indicators; quantify plunger movement visually without presenting invented measurements.

scenes.title: A Scent Spreads
scenes.visualIntent: Use a simple 2D room cross-section showing scent spreading from one side, plus one small particle inset.

scenes.title: Diffusion In Water
scenes.visualIntent: Show a room-temperature clear cup receiving one food-colouring drop; separate a particle-scale diffusion inset from visible current arrows labelled as another possible effect.

checkpoint.prompt: Why should we be cautious about coloured swirls in water?
checkpoint.answer: They may show both diffusion and moving currents.

scenes.title: From Crowded To Spread
scenes.visualIntent: Animate irregular individual paths while a concentration bar graph flattens; keep particles moving after the colour appears evenly distributed.

scenes.title: Predict A New Result
scenes.visualIntent: Present matched shallow and tall clear cups, let a prediction arrow appear, then run a schematic trial with a visible conditions checklist.

scenes.title: What Models Explain
scenes.visualIntent: Build an evidence-to-explanation map linking four investigations to three particle arrangements and diffusion motion; arrows run from evidence towards model claims.

scenes.title: Dots Are Not Portraits
scenes.visualIntent: Reveal only three model conventions: oversized dots, false colour and simplified motion arrows. End with spacing, arrangement and motion as the model's job.

checkpoint.prompt: What three features is this simple model designed to compare?

scenes.title: Repair The Misconceptions
scenes.visualIntent: Display each misconception on an evidence card, strike only the faulty phrase, then rebuild it using reusable spacing, motion and evidence tiles.

scenes.title: Mystery Container
scenes.visualIntent: Use two transparent sealed flexible bags, one with air and one with water; highlight bulging, flow and nearly unchanged liquid volume under gentle pressure.

scenes.title: Design A Fair Check
scenes.visualIntent: Lay out a fair-test bench with adult supervision icon, cool and warm tap-water cups, one-drop dispensers, timer and repeat-trial grid; cross out hot water.

scenes.title: Evidence, Model, Limit
scenes.visualIntent: Complete two three-column response frames for compression and diffusion, then leave a third blank frame ready for the learner's own explanation.

checkpoint.prompt: What are the three parts of a careful particle explanation?
checkpoint.answer: Evidence, model-based explanation, and a model limitation.
